predictions.text: Meta will publicly announce (press release, SEC filing, or CEO post) a workforce reduction totaling at least 10% of its employees.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if Meta discloses layoffs/force reduction explicitly stated as >=10% of employees (or equivalent count implying >=10% using latest reported headcount) in an SEC filing (8-K/10-Q/10-K) or an official company announcement by the deadline.
evidence: TechCrunch reports Meta considering layoffs affecting up to 20% (2026-03-14).
monitoring_signals: Meta 8-K filings and internal memo leaks later confirmed by company
Earnings-call restructuring/expense commentary
WARN notices in key states (CA/WA/NY)
Hiring-freeze or org consolidation announcements
predictions.rationale: Cost-cutting/AI restructuring themes make a sizable reduction plausible, but timing and scale remain uncertain.

predictions.text: Berkshire Hathaway will report more than $5.0 billion of common-stock repurchases in at least one fiscal quarter.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if Berkshire’s Form 10-Q/10-K (or earnings release) shows quarterly common-stock repurchases >$5.0B for any quarter with results released by the deadline.
evidence: CNBC notes Berkshire filing hints at possible share buyback resumption (2026-03-14).
monitoring_signals: Berkshire quarterly filings: repurchase line item
Cash balance and T-bill holdings trends in 10-Q/10-K
Management commentary on valuation and capital deployment
predictions.rationale: Buybacks tend to resume when Buffett/Munger successors view shares as attractive; magnitude depends on market drawdowns and cash needs.

predictions.text: Caesars Entertainment will announce a definitive transaction agreement for an acquisition involving Tilman Fertitta (or a Fertitta-controlled entity).
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if Caesars files an 8-K and/or issues a press release announcing a signed definitive merger/acquisition agreement with Tilman Fertitta or a Fertitta-controlled buyer by the deadline.
evidence: CNBC reports Fertitta in talks to acquire Caesars (2026-03-14).
monitoring_signals: Caesars 8-Ks, deal-rumor confirmations/denials
Regulatory filings (HSR, state gaming approvals) chatter
Financing package rumors (banks/term loans/bonds)
Icahn/public shareholder activity
predictions.rationale: Talks can fail due to financing/regulatory complexity; probability reflects deal risk despite strategic interest.

predictions.text: Bitcoin (BTC) will record at least one daily close at or above $90,000 (USD).
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if CoinMarketCap (or another major aggregator such as CoinGecko) shows BTC-USD daily close >= 90,000 on any day by the deadline.
evidence: CNBC notes bitcoin has outperformed major assets since the war began (2026-03-14).
monitoring_signals: Spot BTC ETF net inflows/outflows
Risk sentiment (VIX, credit spreads) and liquidity conditions
Dollar index (DXY) and real yields
Crypto exchange stablecoin supply growth
predictions.rationale: War-risk and liquidity swings can catalyze sharp moves, but crypto remains highly path-dependent.

predictions.text: OPEC+ will announce a single meeting decision that increases the group’s aggregate production targets/quotas by at least 1.5 million barrels per day versus the immediately prior schedule.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if an official OPEC/OPEC+ communique shows a net increase in stated production targets/quotas totaling >=1.5 mb/d relative to the previously published schedule, decided at one meeting by the deadline.
evidence: News cycle highlights sustained Hormuz risk and large coordinated stock releases, raising pressure for supply responses.
monitoring_signals: OPEC+ meeting calendar and draft communiques
Saudi/UAE signals about spare capacity deployment
Oil price levels and time-spread stress (backwardation)
IEA/OPEC monthly reports on balances
predictions.rationale: If prices/volatility stay elevated, producers may choose a large quota response, though cohesion and politics constrain action.

predictions.text: ICE Brent will show tight prompt fundamentals: the Brent front-month minus second-month settlement spread will be at least $3.00 per barrel (backwardation) on at least one trading day.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if ICE Brent futures settlements show (Front-month settlement − Second-month settlement) >= $3.00/bbl on any trading day by the deadline.
evidence: War-risk and shipping disruption concerns have driven risk premia and tighter near-term supply conditions in recent cycles.
monitoring_signals: Brent 1-2 and 1-6 spreads daily settlements
Physical differentials (Dated Brent, Dubai) and refinery margins
Tanker rates/insurance costs affecting effective supply
SPR/IEA release delivery timing vs demand
predictions.rationale: Even if headline prices are capped, disruptions often express first in prompt spreads.

predictions.text: The U.S. will enact a supplemental appropriation or emergency spending package that provides at least $50 billion in incremental Department of Defense budget authority explicitly justified by the Iran conflict/escalation.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if a bill becomes law by the deadline and the statutory text/official summary provides >=$50B incremental DoD budget authority with stated purpose tied to the Iran conflict/escalation (e.g., operations, munitions replenishment, deployments).
evidence: NYT highlights war spending pressures and fiscal trade-offs (2026-03-14).
monitoring_signals: OMB/White House supplemental requests
House/Senate Armed Services and Appropriations markups
CR/omnibus negotiations including emergency defense titles
DoD reprogramming and readiness/munitions shortfall statements
predictions.rationale: Sustained operations and replenishment needs often drive large supplementals, but politics and deficit concerns may limit size.

predictions.text: U.S. initial jobless claims (seasonally adjusted) will show a 4-week moving average at or above 300,000 for at least one weekly release.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if the U.S. Department of Labor weekly claims release reports the 4-week moving average of seasonally adjusted initial claims >=300,000 in any week by the deadline.
evidence: Risk-off and oil-shock conditions frequently feed into broader financial tightening and hiring slowdown with a lag.
monitoring_signals: Continuing claims trend and insured unemployment rate
Layoff announcements and WARN notices
NFIB hiring plans and PMI employment subcomponents
Credit conditions (SLOOS) and HY spreads
predictions.rationale: A moderate probability reflects the possibility of war/oil-driven tightening, offset by resilience in services and fiscal support.

predictions.text: Airbus will report the first flight of at least one of the two Airbus-prepared uncrewed combat aircraft (from Kratos) referenced in its March 2026 announcement.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if Airbus (or an official partner/government source) issues a press release or public statement confirming first flight of at least one of the two uncrewed combat aircraft referenced in the March 2026 Airbus announcement, by the deadline.
evidence: Airbus stated it is preparing two uncrewed combat aircraft from Kratos for first flight with a European customer (2026-03-14).
monitoring_signals: Airbus Defence & Space press releases
European MoD procurement updates and test-range notices
Airworthiness/test permits and flight-tracking sightings
Program milestone slides in Airbus investor materials
predictions.rationale: Programs often slip, but stated preparation for first flight suggests a reasonable chance within the year.
